preamble: The alveolar lateral approximant /l/ can undergo transformation into a voiceless alveolo-palatal affricate /tʃ/ through processes of palatalization and affrication, often occurring within consonant clusters or as a result of assimilation to a neighboring palatal sound.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, affrication, assimilation, change in manner from approximant to affricate, change in place from alveolar to alveolo-palatal, and devoicing (if the original /l/ was voiced)

examples.note: Latin consonant cluster C'L developing into /tʃ/.
